item.title: Camp Mystic withdraws application for 2026 camp license, will not reopen
item.description: This comes on the heels of the first public hearing over last summer's Fourth of July floods that killed more than 100 people across the Hill Country, including 27 girls at Camp Mystic.

item.title: Video captures massive explosion as NYPD officers approach home, 1 dead
item.description: A body believed to be that of the suspect was recovered after an explosion triggered a five-alarm fire at a home in Queens early Thursday morning.

item.title: Iran war pushes oil prices to 4-year high as Hegseth faces lawmakers
item.description: Oil prices hit a 4-year high as Axios reports Trump will hear new options to try to break the Strait of Hormuz standoff with Iran with a new wave of attacks.

item.title: 2 employees killed in Kentucky bank robbery, suspect at large, police say
item.description: Kentucky State Police said a man went to a U.S. Bank in Brea, Kentucky, and shot and killed a man and a woman, both employees at the bank.

item.title: 5 wounded in possible stabbing attack at Washington high school
item.description: The suspect, a student, was detained at the scene, police said.

item.title: As Iran war nears key 60-day deadline, Congress and Trump face choices
item.description: The War Powers Resolution sets deadlines for the president to end hostilities without congressional approval.

item.title: New White House drug abuse strategy floats wastewater testing, AI, more treatment
item.description: The Trump administration is proposing wastewater testing to try to ferret out data on illegal drug use in real time, according to a draft of a new drug control strategy obtained by CBS News. It also proposes using AI to track threats.

item.title: Hegseth fields Democrats' scrutinizing Iran war questions at combative Senate hearing
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth testified before the Senate Armed Services Committee on Thursday, facing intense questioning from Democrats on the Pentagon budget, the war with Iran and more. These are some of the most notable exchanges from the hearing.

item.title: Breaking down OPEC and its influence as UAE decides to leave
item.description: President Trump gave his endorsement to the United Arab Emirates' decision to leave the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries, or OPEC, on Wednesday. "The Daily Report" breaks it down.

item.title: Why did Saudi Arabia pull its LIV Golf funding?
item.description: LIV Golf is seeking new backers after Saudi Arabia's public investment fund announced it will be pulling its funding at the end of the season. Gabby Herzig, a staff writer at The Athletic, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: CBS News' Fin Gómez reflects on correspondents' dinner shooting: "Incredible force of reporters"
item.description: CBS News political director and executive director of politics and White House Fin Gómez reflects on the White House Correspondents' Dinner attack on "The Daily Report," saying he was proud of "our fellow colleagues."

item.title: Fire aboard Navy destroyer USS Higgins, officials say
item.description: One of the officials told CBS News that the fire knocked out power and propulsion on the guided-missile destroyer, a mainstay of the Navy's forward presence in Asia.

item.title: "It's amazing how resilient consumer spending has been" amid the war, analyst says
item.description: The national average gas price is up to $4.30 per gallon, the highest in four years, as President Trump said Thursday that gas prices "will drop like a rock" when the Iran war ends. Ted Rossman, principal analyst at Bankrate, joins "The Daily Report" with more.

item.title: Trump signs bill funding DHS, ending record-breaking 76-day shutdown
item.description: The longest shutdown of a federal department in U.S. history came to an end on Thursday when President Trump signed a bill to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security following a breakthrough on Capitol Hill.

item.title: Trump signs bill ending DHS shutdown
item.description: The record-long 76-day shutdown at the Department of Homeland Security has ended. President Trump signed a bill on Thursday to fund most of the DHS after it passed the House earlier that day and the Senate last month. CBS News' Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: Confrontation between officers and alleged shooter at D.C. gala lasted 7 seconds
item.description: Seven seconds passed between when the alleged gunman at Saturday's White House Correspondents' Dinner — carrying a shotgun initially concealed by a jacket — first encountered federal law enforcement and when he was subdued, sources told CBS News.

item.title: Oil prices hit wartime peak, with Brent crude touching $126 a barrel
item.description: Brent crude surged past $126 a barrel early Thursday, while U.S. gasoline prices jumped to $4.30 a gallon.

item.title: Florida GOP chair says voters will get "fair elections" due to new congressional map
item.description: Florida is moving forward with Gov. Ron DeSantis' plan to redraw its congressional map that would likely give Republicans more seats. Evan Power, chair of the Florida Republican Party, joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: Texas mother of 4 released from ICE custody after more than a month in detention
item.description: Meenu Batra, a single mother of four adult U.S. citizens, was arrested on March 17 by federal immigration officers while traveling for a work trip.

item.title: 4/30: CBS Evening News
item.description: NYPD officers were injured in an intentional home explosion; D.C. shooting suspect agrees to remain detained until trial.

item.title: New Mexico Gov. Lujan Grisham explains how the state's universal childcare program works
item.description: New Mexico is facing a challenge to its free universal childcare program, the only one of its kind in the U.S. Democratic Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham joins "The Takeout" to break down how it works.

item.title: Ex-federal official worried U.S. "may not come out on top" if Iran war becomes "battle of endurance"
item.description: The ceasefire with Iran is holding, but negotiations are stalled. The ripple effects: tight energy supplies, price spikes and recession risks. Edward Fishman, former Russian and Europe sanctions lead at the State Department, joins with analysis.

item.title: "Ozempic breath" is boosting Hershey's sales of mints and gum
item.description: Hershey says it's benefiting from the growing use of GLP-1 weight-loss drugs even as people cut down on snacks. Here's why.

item.title: Lawyer for Texas mother who spent over a month in ICE detention says "this has broken her"
item.description: A single mother of four is now free after ICE held her in a Texas immigration detention center for more than a month. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul has the latest.

item.title: Rep. James Walkinshaw previews line of questioning for Bondi's Epstein deposition
item.description: Pam Bondi is set to testify late May on the Justice Department's handling of the Jeffrey Epstein files in a closed-door deposition before the House Oversight Committee. Democratic Rep. James Walkinshaw of Virginia joins "The Takeout" to discuss what he wants to hear from the former attorney general.

item.title: 4/30: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Trump signs bill funding Department of Homeland Security, ending 76-day shutdown; Louisiana postpones House primaries after Supreme Court ruling.

item.title: Spotify's new badge identifies human artists, as AI music floods in
item.description: As AI-generated music spreads, Spotify says it wants to help users "trust the authenticity" of what they're listening to.

item.title: Funding cuts for fentanyl test strips raise concerns: "It doesn't make sense"
item.description: Test strips cost about $1 each and can be used to check drugs for dangerous contaminants, including fentanyl and xylazine.

item.title: Tune in: Artemis II astronauts join "CBS Mornings" for a special live hour Friday
item.description: A few weeks back from their trip around the moon, the Artemis crew joins CBS News for "Artemis II: A Celebration of Heroes," a town hall featuring questions from students, and special guests.

item.title: Suspect in D.C. shooting held in "safe cell," agrees to remain jailed ahead of trial
item.description: Cole Allen, 31, is facing three charges related to the attack outside the White House Correspondents' Dinner, including attempting to assassinate President Trump.
